message:
fix(messaging): rebuild dedup index after retention truncation removes records

The stream retention enforcer (enforce_limits) removes old records when
the stream exceeds message-count, byte-size, or max-age limits. However,
the in-memory dedup index was not being pruned after truncation, so
message IDs from records that had already been removed from storage
remained in the dedup window. This caused spurious duplicate rejections:
a producer that re-sent a message after its earlier copy was legitimately
evicted by retention would get a dedup rejection even though the original
was gone.

The fix tracks whether any truncation occurred during the enforce_limits
loop and, if so, rebuilds the dedup index from the current storage
snapshot before returning. This ensures the dedup window always reflects
what is actually stored, not what was historically appended.

Test added: dedup_window_forgets_records_truncated_by_retention — appends
two messages under a max_msgs=1 policy, verifies the first is evicted,
then re-appends a message with the same content and confirms it is
accepted rather than rejected as a duplicate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
